88	class ConfigMixin:
89	    r"""
90	    Base class for all configuration classes. All configuration parameters are stored under `self.config`. Also
91	    provides the [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] and [`~ConfigMixin.save_config`] methods for loading, downloading, and
92	    saving classes that inherit from [`ConfigMixin`].
93	
94	    Class attributes:
95	        - **config_name** (`str`) -- A filename under which the config should stored when calling
96	          [`~ConfigMixin.save_config`] (should be overridden by parent class).
97	        - **ignore_for_config** (`list[str]`) -- A list of attributes that should not be saved in the config (should be
98	          overridden by subclass).
99	        - **has_compatibles** (`bool`) -- Whether the class has compatible classes (should be overridden by subclass).
100	        - **_deprecated_kwargs** (`list[str]`) -- Keyword arguments that are deprecated. Note that the `init` function
101	          should only have a `kwargs` argument if at least one argument is deprecated (should be overridden by
102	          subclass).
103	    """
104	
105	    config_name = None
106	    ignore_for_config = []
107	    has_compatibles = False
108	
109	    _deprecated_kwargs = []
110	    _auto_class = None
111	
112	    @classmethod
113	    def register_for_auto_class(cls, auto_class="AutoModel"):
114	        """
115	        Register this class with the given auto class so that it can be loaded with `AutoModel.from_pretrained(...,
116	        trust_remote_code=True)`.
117	
118	        When the config is saved, the resulting `config.json` will include an `auto_map` entry mapping the auto class
119	        to this class's module and class name.
120	
121	        Args:
122	            auto_class (`str` or type, *optional*, defaults to `"AutoModel"`):
123	                The auto class to register this class with. Can be a string (e.g. `"AutoModel"`) or the class itself.
124	                Currently only `"AutoModel"` is supported.
125	
126	        Example:
127	
128	        ```python
129	        from diffusers import ModelMixin, ConfigMixin
130	
131	
132	        class MyCustomModel(ModelMixin, ConfigMixin): ...
133	
134	
135	        MyCustomModel.register_for_auto_class("AutoModel")
136	        ```
137	        """
138	        if auto_class != "AutoModel":
139	            raise ValueError(f"Only 'AutoModel' is supported, got '{auto_class}'.")
140	
141	        cls._auto_class = auto_class
142	
143	    def register_to_config(self, **kwargs):
144	        if self.config_name is None:
145	            raise NotImplementedError(f"Make sure that {self.__class__} has defined a class name `config_name`")
146	        # Special case for `kwargs` used in deprecation warning added to schedulers
147	        # TODO: remove this when we remove the deprecation warning, and the `kwargs` argument,
148	        # or solve in a more general way.
149	        kwargs.pop("kwargs", None)
150	
151	        if not hasattr(self, "_internal_dict"):
152	            internal_dict = kwargs
153	        else:
154	            previous_dict = dict(self._internal_dict)
155	            internal_dict = {**self._internal_dict, **kwargs}
156	            logger.debug(f"Updating config from {previous_dict} to {internal_dict}")
157	
158	        self._internal_dict = FrozenDict(internal_dict)
159	
160	    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
161	        """The only reason we overwrite `getattr` here is to gracefully deprecate accessing
162	        config attributes directly. See https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers/pull/3129
163	
164	        This function is mostly copied from PyTorch's __getattr__ overwrite:
165	        https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/_modules/torch/nn/modules/module.html#Module
166	        """
167	
168	        is_in_config = "_internal_dict" in self.__dict__ and hasattr(self.__dict__["_internal_dict"], name)
169	        is_attribute = name in self.__dict__
170	
171	        if is_in_config and not is_attribute:
172	            deprecation_message = f"Accessing config attribute `{name}` directly via '{type(self).__name__}' object attribute is deprecated. Please access '{name}' over '{type(self).__name__}'s config object instead, e.g. 'scheduler.config.{name}'."
173	            deprecate("direct config name access", "1.0.0", deprecation_message, standard_warn=False)
174	            return self._internal_dict[name]
175	
176	        raise AttributeError(f"'{type(self).__name__}' object has no attribute '{name}'")
177	
178	    def save_config(self, save_directory: str | os.PathLike, push_to_hub: bool = False, **kwargs):
179	        """
180	        Save a configuration object to the directory specified in `save_directory` so that it can be reloaded using the
181	        [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] class method.
182	
183	        Args:
184	            save_directory (`str` or `os.PathLike`):
185	                Directory where the configuration JSON file is saved (will be created if it does not exist).
186	            push_to_hub (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
187	                Whether or not to push your model to the Hugging Face Hub after saving it. You can specify the
188	                repository you want to push to with `repo_id` (will default to the name of `save_directory` in your
189	                namespace).
190	            kwargs (`dict[str, Any]`, *optional*):
191	                Additional keyword arguments passed along to the [`~utils.PushToHubMixin.push_to_hub`] method.
192	        """
193	        if os.path.isfile(save_directory):
194	            raise AssertionError(f"Provided path ({save_directory}) should be a directory, not a file")
195	
196	        os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)
197	
198	        # If we save using the predefined names, we can load using `from_config`
199	        output_config_file = os.path.join(save_directory, self.config_name)
200	
201	        self.to_json_file(output_config_file)
202	        logger.info(f"Configuration saved in {output_config_file}")
203	
204	        if push_to_hub:
205	            commit_message = kwargs.pop("commit_message", None)
206	            private = kwargs.pop("private", None)
207	            create_pr = kwargs.pop("create_pr", False)
208	            token = kwargs.pop("token", None)
209	            repo_id = kwargs.pop("repo_id", save_directory.split(os.path.sep)[-1])
210	            repo_id = create_repo(repo_id, exist_ok=True, private=private, token=token).repo_id
211	            subfolder = kwargs.pop("subfolder", None)
212	
213	            self._upload_folder(
214	                save_directory,
215	                repo_id,
216	                token=token,
217	                commit_message=commit_message,
218	                create_pr=create_pr,
219	                subfolder=subfolder,
220	            )

690	def register_to_config(init):
691	    r"""
692	    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
693	    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
694	    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable
695	
696	    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
697	    """
698	
699	    @functools.wraps(init)
700	    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
701	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
702	        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
703	        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
704	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
705	            raise RuntimeError(
706	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
707	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
708	            )
709	
710	        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
711	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
712	        new_kwargs = {}
713	        signature = inspect.signature(init)
714	        parameters = {
715	            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) if i > 0 and name not in ignore
716	        }
717	        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
718	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
719	
720	        # Then add all kwargs
721	        new_kwargs.update(
722	            {
723	                k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
724	                for k, default in parameters.items()
725	                if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
726	            }
727	        )
728	
729	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
730	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
731	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
732	
733	        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
734	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
735	        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
736	
737	    return inner_init
738	
739	
740	def flax_register_to_config(cls):
741	    original_init = cls.__init__
742	
743	    @functools.wraps(original_init)
744	    def init(self, *args, **kwargs):
745	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
746	            raise RuntimeError(
747	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
748	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
749	            )
750	
751	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init. Retrieve all passed attributes
752	        init_kwargs = dict(kwargs.items())
753	
754	        # Retrieve default values
755	        fields = dataclasses.fields(self)
756	        default_kwargs = {}
757	        for field in fields:
758	            # ignore flax specific attributes
759	            if field.name in self._flax_internal_args:
760	                continue
761	            if type(field.default) == dataclasses._MISSING_TYPE:
762	                default_kwargs[field.name] = None
763	            else:
764	                default_kwargs[field.name] = getattr(self, field.name)
765	
766	        # Make sure init_kwargs override default kwargs
767	        new_kwargs = {**default_kwargs, **init_kwargs}
768	        # dtype should be part of `init_kwargs`, but not `new_kwargs`
769	        if "dtype" in new_kwargs:
770	            new_kwargs.pop("dtype")
771	
772	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
773	        for i, arg in enumerate(args):
774	            name = fields[i].name
775	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
776	
777	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
778	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
779	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
780	
781	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
782	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
783	
784	    cls.__init__ = init
785	    return cls

40	class BaseOutput(OrderedDict):
41	    """
42	    Base class for all model outputs as dataclass. Has a `__getitem__` that allows indexing by integer or slice (like a
43	    tuple) or strings (like a dictionary) that will ignore the `None` attributes. Otherwise behaves like a regular
44	    Python dictionary.
45	
46	    > [!WARNING] > You can't unpack a [`BaseOutput`] directly. Use the [`~utils.BaseOutput.to_tuple`] method to convert
47	    it to a tuple > first.

50	    def __init_subclass__(cls) -> None:
51	        """Register subclasses as pytree nodes.
52	
53	        This is necessary to synchronize gradients when using `torch.nn.parallel.DistributedDataParallel` with
54	        `static_graph=True` with modules that output `ModelOutput` subclasses.
55	        """
56	        if is_torch_available():
57	            import torch.utils._pytree
58	
59	            if is_torch_version("<", "2.2"):
60	                torch.utils._pytree._register_pytree_node(
61	                    cls,
62	                    torch.utils._pytree._dict_flatten,
63	                    lambda values, context: cls(**torch.utils._pytree._dict_unflatten(values, context)),
64	                )
65	            else:
66	                torch.utils._pytree.register_pytree_node(
67	                    cls,
68	                    torch.utils._pytree._dict_flatten,
69	                    lambda values, context: cls(**torch.utils._pytree._dict_unflatten(values, context)),
70	                    serialized_type_name=f"{cls.__module__}.{cls.__name__}",
71	                )

73	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
74	        class_fields = fields(self)
75	
76	        # Safety and consistency checks
77	        if not len(class_fields):
78	            raise ValueError(f"{self.__class__.__name__} has no fields.")
79	
80	        first_field = getattr(self, class_fields[0].name)
81	        other_fields_are_none = all(getattr(self, field.name) is None for field in class_fields[1:])
82	
83	        if other_fields_are_none and isinstance(first_field, dict):
84	            for key, value in first_field.items():
85	                self[key] = value
86	        else:
87	            for field in class_fields:
88	                v = getattr(self, field.name)
89	                if v is not None:
90	                    self[field.name] = v

92	    def __delitem__(self, *args, **kwargs):

95	    def setdefault(self, *args, **kwargs):

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary report with all the key findings:

## Thorough Exploration Report: Hugging Face Diffusers Scheduler Patterns

Based on my complete end-to-end analysis of the Hugging Face diffusers library, here are the comprehensive findings on scheduler patterns:

### Directory Structure
- **Schedulers directory**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/`
- **50+ scheduler implementations** available (DDPM, DDIM, Euler, DPM Solver variants, etc.)

---

## 1. DDPMScheduler Full Implementation Pattern

**File Path**: `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py`

### Output Dataclass (Lines 30-45)
```python
@dataclass
class DDPMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None
```

### Imports (Lines 24-27)
```python
from ..configuration_utils import ConfigMixin, register_to_config
from ..utils import BaseOutput
from ..utils.torch_utils import randn_tensor
from .scheduling_utils import KarrasDiffusionSchedulers, SchedulerMixin
```

### Class Definition & Inheritance (Lines 137-141)
```python
class DDPMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
    order = 1
```

### @register_to_config Decorator Usage (Lines 186-211)
```python
@register_to_config
def __init__(
    self,
    num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
    beta_start: float = 0.0001,
    beta_end: float = 0.02,
    beta_schedule: Literal["linear", "scaled_linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2", "sigmoid"] = "linear",
    trained_betas: np.ndarray | list[float] | None = None,
    variance_type: Literal[...] = "fixed_small",
    clip_sample: bool = True,
    prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "epsilon",
    thresholding: bool = False,
    dynamic_thresholding_ratio: float = 0.995,
    clip_sample_range: float = 1.0,
    sample_max_value: float = 1.0,
    timestep_spacing: Literal["linspace", "leading", "trailing"] = "leading",
    steps_offset: int = 0,
    rescale_betas_zero_snr: bool = False,
):
```

**Key Pattern**: The decorator automatically calls `self.register_to_config(**kwargs)` internally (lines 700-735 of configuration_utils.py), storing all parameters in `self._internal_dict` as a `FrozenDict`.

### set_timesteps Method (Lines 274-346)
- Takes `num_inference_steps`, `device`, and optional custom `timesteps`
- Validates custom timesteps are in descending order
- Implements three spacing strategies: `linspace`, `leading`, `trailing`
- Sets `self.timesteps` as a torch tensor on the specified device
- Handles both custom timesteps and auto-generated spacing

### step Method (Lines 461-567)
```python
def step(
    self,
    model_output: torch.Tensor,
    timestep: int,
    sample: torch.Tensor,
    generator: torch.Generator | None = None,
    return_dict: bool = True,
) -> DDPMSchedulerOutput | tuple:
```
- Returns `DDPMSchedulerOutput` dataclass or tuple based on `return_dict`
- Line 567: `return DDPMSchedulerOutput(prev_sample=pred_prev_sample, pred_original_sample=pred_original_sample)`

### add_noise Method (Lines 569-609)
```python
def add_noise(
    self,
    original_samples: torch.Tensor,
    noise: torch.Tensor,
    timesteps: torch.IntTensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
```
- Forward diffusion process
- Uses `self.alphas_cumprod` indexed by timesteps

### Helper Methods
- `scale_model_input()` (line 257): Identity for DDPM, overridden in others
- `_get_variance()` (line 348): Computes variance based on type
- `_threshold_sample()` (line 418): Dynamic thresholding for stability
- `get_velocity()` (line 611): Computes velocity from sample and noise
- `previous_timestep()` (line 648): Gets previous timestep from index

---

## 2. DDIMScheduler Pattern Validation

**File Path**: `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py`

### Consistent Pattern Confirmation (Lines 139-211)
```python
class DDIMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
    order = 1

    @register_to_config
    def __init__(self, ...):
```

### DDIMSchedulerOutput (Lines 31-47)
Identical pattern to DDPM:
```python
@dataclass
class DDIMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None
```

### Key Differences
- `set_timesteps()` (line 334): No custom timesteps support, only `num_inference_steps`
- `_get_variance()` (line 262): Specialized variance computation for DDIM
- `step()` (lines 384-514): DDIM-specific noise schedule with eta parameter (line 389)
- Both `add_noise()` and `get_velocity()` are **Copied from DDPM** (marked at lines 516, 559)

---

## 3. EulerDiscreteScheduler Pattern

**File Path**: `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_euler_discrete.py`

### Pattern (Lines 143-275)
```python
class EulerDiscreteScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
    order = 1

    @register_to_config
    def __init__(self, ...):
```

### Key Differences from DDPM/DDIM
- Uses **sigma-based scheduling** instead of alpha/beta (line 253): `sigmas = (((1 - self.alphas_cumprod) / self.alphas_cumprod) ** 0.5).flip(0)`
- Properties for state tracking:
  - `init_noise_sigma` property (lines 277-291)
  - `step_index` property (lines 293-302)
  - `begin_index` property (lines 304-313)
- **set_timesteps** (lines 350-400) supports three input modes:
  - `num_inference_steps`: Auto-generate schedule
  - Custom `timesteps`: User-provided
  - Custom `sigmas`: User-provided with derived timesteps
- **scale_model_input()** (lines 326-348): **Non-identity** - divides by `(sigma**2 + 1) ** 0.5`
- `set_begin_index()` (line 316): Specific to Euler for pipeline integration

---

## 4. Base Classes & Configuration System

### SchedulerMixin (Lines 74-189 of scheduling_utils.py)
- Provides `from_pretrained()` and `save_pretrained()` methods
- Inherits from `PushToHubMixin` for Hub integration
- Class attributes:
  - `config_name = "scheduler_config.json"`
  - `_compatibles = []` (list of compatible scheduler class names)
  - `has_compatibles = True`

### ConfigMixin (Lines 88-300 of configuration_utils.py)
- Stores config in `self._internal_dict` as `FrozenDict`
- `@register_to_config` decorator (lines 690-737):
  - Wraps `__init__` method
  - Extracts parameters from args/kwargs based on signature
  - Calls `self.register_to_config(**new_kwargs)` automatically
  - Ignores attributes in `ignore_for_config` list
  - Filters out private kwargs (starting with `_`)
- Instance method `register_to_config()` (lines 143-157):
  - Stores kwargs in `self._internal_dict`
  - Creates a frozen config dictionary
- Access pattern: `self.config.parameter_name` returns the stored value

### SchedulerOutput Base Class (Lines 60-71 of scheduling_utils.py)
```python
@dataclass
class SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
```
- Generic base for all scheduler outputs
- Most schedulers extend this with additional fields

---

## 5. BaseOutput Implementation

**File Path**: `/src/diffusers/utils/outputs.py` (Lines 40-134)

```python
class BaseOutput(OrderedDict):
    """Base class for all model outputs as dataclass."""
```

### Key Characteristics (Lines 40-134)
- Inherits from `OrderedDict`
- Registers subclasses as PyTorch pytree nodes (lines 50-71)
- `__post_init__()` (lines 73-90): Populates dict from dataclass fields
- Dict-like access with string keys and tuple-like access with indices
- Immutable: prevents `__delitem__`, `setdefault`, `pop`, `update`
- `to_tuple()` method (lines 130-134): Converts to tuple for unpacking
- Used with `@dataclass` decorator on all output classes

---

## 6. Consistent Pattern Summary Across All Schedulers

| Aspect | Pattern |
|--------|---------|
| **Class Definition** | `class XyzScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin)` |
| **Init Decorator** | `@register_to_config` on `__init__` method |
| **Output Dataclass** | `@dataclass class XyzSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput)` with at least `prev_sample: torch.Tensor` |
| **Core Methods** | `set_timesteps()`, `step()`, `scale_model_input()`, `add_noise()`, `get_velocity()` |
| **Config Access** | `self.config.parameter_name` for stored parameters |
| **Return Types** | Either specific output dataclass or tuple based on `return_dict=True` |
| **Timestep Index** | Some use explicit `previous_timestep()`, others track `_step_index` as property |
| **Beta/Sigma Schedule** | Computed in `__init__`, stored as `self.alphas_cumprod` or `self.sigmas` |

---

## 7. File Path Summary

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| DDPMScheduler | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py` (lines 137-669) |
| DDIMScheduler | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py` (lines 139-596) |
| EulerDiscreteScheduler | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_euler_discrete.py` (lines 143-...) |
| SchedulerMixin | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py` (lines 74-189) |
| SchedulerOutput | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py` (lines 60-71) |
| ConfigMixin | `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py` (lines 88-300+) |
| @register_to_config decorator | `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py` (lines 690-737) |
| BaseOutput | `/src/diffusers/utils/outputs.py` (lines 40-134) |
| Schedulers Init | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py` (lines 40-100+) |

---

This pattern is remarkably consistent across 50+ scheduler implementations, making the codebase highly maintainable and extensible.

- Instance method `register_to_config()` (lines 143-157):
  - Stores kwargs in `self._internal_dict`
  - Creates a frozen config dictionary
- Access pattern: `self.config.parameter_name` returns the stored value

### SchedulerOutput Base Class (Lines 60-71 of scheduling_utils.py)
```python
@dataclass
class SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
```
- Generic base for all scheduler outputs
- Most schedulers extend this with additional fields

---

## 5. BaseOutput Implementation

**File Path**: `/src/diffusers/utils/outputs.py` (Lines 40-134)

```python
class BaseOutput(OrderedDict):
    """Base class for all model outputs as dataclass."""
```

### Key Characteristics (Lines 40-134)
- Inherits from `OrderedDict`
- Registers subclasses as PyTorch pytree nodes (lines 50-71)
- `__post_init__()` (lines 73-90): Populates dict from dataclass fields
- Dict-like access with string keys and tuple-like access with indices
- Immutable: prevents `__delitem__`, `setdefault`, `pop`, `update`
- `to_tuple()` method (lines 130-134): Converts to tuple for unpacking
- Used with `@dataclass` decorator on all output classes

---

## 6. Consistent Pattern Summary Across All Schedulers

| Aspect | Pattern |
|--------|---------|
| **Class Definition** | `class XyzScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin)` |
| **Init Decorator** | `@register_to_config` on `__init__` method |
| **Output Dataclass** | `@dataclass class XyzSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput)` with at least `prev_sample: torch.Tensor` |
| **Core Methods** | `set_timesteps()`, `step()`, `scale_model_input()`, `add_noise()`, `get_velocity()` |
| **Config Access** | `self.config.parameter_name` for stored parameters |
| **Return Types** | Either specific output dataclass or tuple based on `return_dict=True` |
| **Timestep Index** | Some use explicit `previous_timestep()`, others track `_step_index` as property |
| **Beta/Sigma Schedule** | Computed in `__init__`, stored as `self.alphas_cumprod` or `self.sigmas` |

---

## 7. File Path Summary

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| DDPMScheduler | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py` (lines 137-669) |
| DDIMScheduler | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py` (lines 139-596) |
| EulerDiscreteScheduler | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_euler_discrete.py` (lines 143-...) |
| SchedulerMixin | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py` (lines 74-189) |
| SchedulerOutput | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py` (lines 60-71) |
| ConfigMixin | `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py` (lines 88-300+) |
| @register_to_config decorator | `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py` (lines 690-737) |
| BaseOutput | `/src/diffusers/utils/outputs.py` (lines 40-134) |
| Schedulers Init | `/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py` (lines 40-100+) |

---

This pattern is remarkably consistent across 50+ scheduler implementations, making the codebase highly maintainable and extensible.

Very thorough exploration of the testing patterns for schedulers in the Hugging Face diffusers library. I need to understand how to write tests for a new scheduler.

Please investigate:

1. Find the test directory for schedulers — likely `tests/schedulers/`
2. List all test files there
3. Read one complete test file for a simple scheduler (e.g., `test_scheduler_ddpm.py` or `test_scheduler_ddim.py`) — I need to see:
   - The full test class structure
   - What base test class is used (e.g., `SchedulerCommonTest`)

   - What `get_scheduler_config` returns
   - Required test methods
4. Read the base test class (likely in `tests/schedulers/test_schedulers.py` or similar) to understand what common tests exist

358	    def dummy_model(self):
359	        def model(sample, t, *args):
360	            # if t is a tensor, match the number of dimensions of sample
361	            if isinstance(t, torch.Tensor):
362	                num_dims = len(sample.shape)
363	                # pad t with 1s to match num_dims
364	                t = t.reshape(-1, *(1,) * (num_dims - 1)).to(sample.device, dtype=sample.dtype)
365	
366	            return sample * t / (t + 1)
367	
368	        return model

370	    def check_over_configs(self, time_step=0, **config):
371	        kwargs = dict(self.forward_default_kwargs)
372	
373	        num_inference_steps = kwargs.pop("num_inference_steps", None)
374	        time_step = time_step if time_step is not None else self.default_timestep
375	
376	        for scheduler_class in self.scheduler_classes:
377	            # TODO(Suraj) - delete the following two lines once DDPM, DDIM, and PNDM have timesteps casted to float by default
378	            if scheduler_class in (EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler, LMSDiscreteScheduler):
379	                time_step = float(time_step)
380	
381	            scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
382	            scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
383	
384	            if scheduler_class == CMStochasticIterativeScheduler:
385	                # Get valid timestep based on sigma_max, which should always be in timestep schedule.
386	                scaled_sigma_max = scheduler.sigma_to_t(scheduler.config.sigma_max)
387	                time_step = scaled_sigma_max
388	
389	            if scheduler_class == EDMEulerScheduler:
390	                time_step = scheduler.timesteps[-1]
391	
392	            if scheduler_class == VQDiffusionScheduler:
393	                num_vec_classes = scheduler_config["num_vec_classes"]
394	                sample = self.dummy_sample(num_vec_classes)
395	                model = self.dummy_model(num_vec_classes)
396	                residual = model(sample, time_step)
397	            else:
398	                sample = self.dummy_sample
399	                residual = 0.1 * sample
400	
401	            with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdirname:
402	                scheduler.save_config(tmpdirname)
403	                new_scheduler = scheduler_class.from_pretrained(tmpdirname)
404	
405	            if num_inference_steps is not None and hasattr(scheduler, "set_timesteps"):
406	                scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
407	                new_scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
408	            elif num_inference_steps is not None and not hasattr(scheduler, "set_timesteps"):
409	                kwargs["num_inference_steps"] = num_inference_steps
410	
411	            # Make sure `scale_model_input` is invoked to prevent a warning
412	            if scheduler_class == CMStochasticIterativeScheduler:
413	                # Get valid timestep based on sigma_max, which should always be in timestep schedule.
414	                _ = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scaled_sigma_max)
415	                _ = new_scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scaled_sigma_max)
416	            elif scheduler_class != VQDiffusionScheduler:
417	                _ = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scheduler.timesteps[-1])
418	                _ = new_scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scheduler.timesteps[-1])
419	
420	            # Set the seed before step() as some schedulers are stochastic like EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler
421	            if "generator" in set(inspect.signature(scheduler.step).parameters.keys()):
422	                kwargs["generator"] = torch.manual_seed(0)
423	            output = scheduler.step(residual, time_step, sample, **kwargs).prev_sample
424	
425	            if "generator" in set(inspect.signature(scheduler.step).parameters.keys()):
426	                kwargs["generator"] = torch.manual_seed(0)
427	            new_output = new_scheduler.step(residual, time_step, sample, **kwargs).prev_sample
428	
429	            assert torch.sum(torch.abs(output - new_output)) < 1e-5, "Scheduler outputs are not identical"
430

1	import torch
2	
3	from diffusers import DDIMScheduler
4	
5	from .test_schedulers import SchedulerCommonTest
6	
7	
8	class DDIMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
9	    scheduler_classes = (DDIMScheduler,)
10	    forward_default_kwargs = (("eta", 0.0), ("num_inference_steps", 50))
11	
12	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
13	        config = {
14	            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
15	            "beta_start": 0.0001,
16	            "beta_end": 0.02,
17	            "beta_schedule": "linear",
18	            "clip_sample": True,
19	        }
20	
21	        config.update(**kwargs)
22	        return config
23	
24	    def full_loop(self, **config):
25	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
26	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
27	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
28	
29	        num_inference_steps, eta = 10, 0.0
30	
31	        model = self.dummy_model()
32	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
33	
34	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
35	
36	        for t in scheduler.timesteps:
37	            residual = model(sample, t)
38	            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, eta).prev_sample
39	
40	        return sample
41	
42	    def test_timesteps(self):
43	        for timesteps in [100, 500, 1000]:
44	            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
45	
46	    def test_steps_offset(self):
47	        for steps_offset in [0, 1]:
48	            self.check_over_configs(steps_offset=steps_offset)
49	
50	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
51	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(steps_offset=1)
52	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
53	        scheduler.set_timesteps(5)
54	        assert torch.equal(scheduler.timesteps, torch.LongTensor([801, 601, 401, 201, 1]))
55	
56	    def test_betas(self):
57	        for beta_start, beta_end in zip([0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1], [0.002, 0.02, 0.2, 2]):
58	            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)
59	
60	    def test_schedules(self):
61	        for schedule in ["linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]:
62	            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
63	
64	    def test_prediction_type(self):
65	        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "v_prediction"]:
66	            self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)
67	
68	    def test_clip_sample(self):
69	        for clip_sample in [True, False]:
70	            self.check_over_configs(clip_sample=clip_sample)
71	
72	    def test_timestep_spacing(self):
73	        for timestep_spacing in ["trailing", "leading"]:
74	            self.check_over_configs(timestep_spacing=timestep_spacing)
75	
76	    def test_rescale_betas_zero_snr(self):
77	        for rescale_betas_zero_snr in [True, False]:
78	            self.check_over_configs(rescale_betas_zero_snr=rescale_betas_zero_snr)
79	
80	    def test_thresholding(self):

85	                    thresholding=True,

88	                )

96	            self.check_over_forward(time_step=t, num_inference_steps=num_inference_steps)

102	    def test_variance(self):
103	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
104	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
105	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
106	
107	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(0, 0) - 0.0)) < 1e-5
108	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(420, 400) - 0.14771)) < 1e-5
109	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(980, 960) - 0.32460)) < 1e-5
110	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(0, 0) - 0.0)) < 1e-5
111	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(487, 486) - 0.00979)) < 1e-5
112	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(999, 998) - 0.02)) < 1e-5
113	
114	    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
115	        sample = self.full_loop()
116	
117	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
118	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
119	
120	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 172.0067) < 1e-2
121	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.223967) < 1e-3
122	
123	    def test_full_loop_with_v_prediction(self):
124	        sample = self.full_loop(prediction_type="v_prediction")
125	
126	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
127	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
128	
129	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 52.5302) < 1e-2
130	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.0684) < 1e-3
131	
132	    def test_full_loop_with_set_alpha_to_one(self):
133	        # We specify different beta, so that the first alpha is 0.99
134	        sample = self.full_loop(set_alpha_to_one=True, beta_start=0.01)
135	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
136	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
137	
138	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 149.8295) < 1e-2
139	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.1951) < 1e-3
140	
141	    def test_full_loop_with_no_set_alpha_to_one(self):
142	        # We specify different beta, so that the first alpha is 0.99
143	        sample = self.full_loop(set_alpha_to_one=False, beta_start=0.01)

146	
147	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 149.0784) < 1e-2
148	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.1941) < 1e-3
149	
150	    def test_full_loop_with_noise(self):
151	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
152	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
153	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
154	
155	        num_inference_steps, eta = 10, 0.0
156	        t_start = 8
157	
158	        model = self.dummy_model()
159	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
160	
161	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
162	
163	        # add noise
164	        noise = self.dummy_noise_deter
165	        timesteps = scheduler.timesteps[t_start * scheduler.order :]
166	        sample = scheduler.add_noise(sample, noise, timesteps[:1])
167	
168	        for t in timesteps:
169	            residual = model(sample, t)
170	            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, eta).prev_sample
171	
172	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
173	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
174	
175	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 354.5418) < 1e-2, f" expected result sum 218.4379, but get {result_sum}"
176	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.4616) < 1e-3, f" expected result mean 0.2844, but get {result_mean}"

32	# NOTE: We make this type an enum because it simplifies usage in docs and prevents
33	# circular imports when used for `_compatibles` within the schedulers module.
34	# When it's used as a type in pipelines, it really is a Union because the actual
35	# scheduler instance is passed in.
36	class FlaxKarrasDiffusionSchedulers(Enum):
37	    FlaxDDIMScheduler = 1
38	    FlaxDDPMScheduler = 2
39	    FlaxPNDMScheduler = 3

45	@dataclass
46	class FlaxSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
47	    """
48	    Base class for the scheduler's step function output.
49	
50	    Args:
51	        prev_sample (`jnp.ndarray` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
52	            Computed sample (x_{t-1}) of previous timestep. `prev_sample` should be used as next model input in the
53	            denoising loop.
54	    """
55	
56	    prev_sample: jnp.ndarray
57	
58	
59	class FlaxSchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
60	    """
61	    Mixin containing common functions for the schedulers.
62	
63	    Class attributes:
64	        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of classes that are compatible with the parent class, so that
65	          `from_config` can be used from a class different than the one used to save the config (should be overridden
66	          by parent class).
67	    """
68	
69	    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME
70	    ignore_for_config = ["dtype"]
71	    _compatibles = []
72	    has_compatibles = True
73	
74	    @classmethod
75	    @validate_hf_hub_args
76	    def from_pretrained(
77	        cls,
78	        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
79	        subfolder: str | None = None,

81	        **kwargs,
82	    ):
83	        r"""
84	        Instantiate a Scheduler class from a pre-defined JSON-file.
85	
86	        Parameters:
87	            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
88	                Can be either:
89	
90	                    - A string, the *model id* of a model repo on huggingface.co. Valid model ids should have an
91	                      organization name, like `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`.
92	                    - A path to a *directory* containing model weights saved using [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`],
93	                      e.g., `./my_model_directory/`.
94	            subfolder (`str`, *optional*):
95	                In case the relevant files are located inside a subfolder of the model repo (either remote in
96	                huggingface.co or downloaded locally), you can specify the folder name here.
97	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
98	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
99	
100	            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):

9	class EulerDiscreteSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
10	    scheduler_classes = (EulerDiscreteScheduler,)
11	    num_inference_steps = 10
12	
13	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
14	        config = {
15	            "num_train_timesteps": 1100,
16	            "beta_start": 0.0001,
17	            "beta_end": 0.02,
18	            "beta_schedule": "linear",
19	        }
20	
21	        config.update(**kwargs)
22	        return config
23	
24	    def test_timesteps(self):
25	        for timesteps in [10, 50, 100, 1000]:
26	            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
27	
28	    def test_betas(self):
29	        for beta_start, beta_end in zip([0.00001, 0.0001, 0.001], [0.0002, 0.002, 0.02]):
30	            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)
31	
32	    def test_schedules(self):
33	        for schedule in ["linear", "scaled_linear"]:
34	            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
35	
36	    def test_prediction_type(self):
37	        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "v_prediction"]:
38	            self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)
39	
40	    def test_timestep_type(self):
41	        timestep_types = ["discrete", "continuous"]
42	        for timestep_type in timestep_types:
43	            self.check_over_configs(timestep_type=timestep_type)
44	
45	    def test_karras_sigmas(self):
46	        self.check_over_configs(use_karras_sigmas=True, sigma_min=0.02, sigma_max=700.0)
47	
48	    def test_rescale_betas_zero_snr(self):
49	        for rescale_betas_zero_snr in [True, False]:
50	            self.check_over_configs(rescale_betas_zero_snr=rescale_betas_zero_snr)
51	
52	    def full_loop(self, **config):
53	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
54	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
55	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
56	
57	        num_inference_steps = self.num_inference_steps
58	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
59	
60	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
61	
62	        model = self.dummy_model()
63	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter * scheduler.init_noise_sigma
64	        sample = sample.to(torch_device)
65	
66	        for i, t in enumerate(scheduler.timesteps):
67	            sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, t)
68	
69	            model_output = model(sample, t)
70	
71	            output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample, generator=generator)
72	            sample = output.prev_sample
73	        return sample
74	
75	    def full_loop_custom_timesteps(self, **config):
76	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
77	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
78	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
79	
80	        num_inference_steps = self.num_inference_steps
81	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
82	        timesteps = scheduler.timesteps
83	        # reset the timesteps using `timesteps`
84	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
85	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=None, timesteps=timesteps)
86	
87	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
88	
89	        model = self.dummy_model()
90	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter * scheduler.init_noise_sigma
91	        sample = sample.to(torch_device)
92	
93	        for i, t in enumerate(scheduler.timesteps):
94	            sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, t)
95	
96	            model_output = model(sample, t)
97	
98	            output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample, generator=generator)
99	            sample = output.prev_sample
100	        return sample
101	
102	    def full_loop_custom_sigmas(self, **config):
103	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
104	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
105	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
106	
107	        num_inference_steps = self.num_inference_steps

111	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)

114	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the complete report:

## COMPREHENSIVE SCHEDULER REGISTRATION & EXPORT REPORT

I've conducted a thorough investigation of the Hugging Face diffusers library scheduler registration system. Here's the complete breakdown of every file and exact modifications needed when adding a new scheduler:

---

## FILES THAT NEED MODIFICATION (in order)

### 1. **Scheduler Implementation File**
**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_*.py`

**Example:** `src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py`

This is where your actual scheduler class lives. Key imports needed:
```python
from .scheduling_utils import KarrasDiffusionSchedulers, SchedulerMixin
from ..configuration_utils import ConfigMixin, register_to_config
```

---

### 2. **Schedulers Package Init File** (CRITICAL - 3 SECTIONS)
**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py`

This uses a **lazy import mechanism** with `_import_structure` dictionary. You must add entries in THREE places:

#### **Section A: Lazy Import Structure (Lines 32-130)**
This determines what gets loaded when needed:

**For PyTorch schedulers (requires `is_torch_available()`):**
- **Lines 40-82:** Basic torch scheduler mappings
- **Pattern:**
  ```python
  _import_structure["scheduling_YOUR_SCHEDULER_NAME"] = ["YourSchedulerClass"]
  ```
- **Example (from file):**
  ```python
  _import_structure["scheduling_ddim"] = ["DDIMScheduler"]
  ```
- **Location context:** After line 41 (after the `_import_structure["deprecated"]` line) in alphabetical order

**For Flax schedulers (requires `is_flax_available()`):**
- **Lines 91-105:** Flax scheduler mappings
- **Pattern:**
  ```python
  _import_structure["scheduling_YOUR_SCHEDULER_NAME_flax"] = ["FlaxYourSchedulerClass"]
  ```
- **Example (from file):**
  ```python
  _import_structure["scheduling_ddim_flax"] = ["FlaxDDIMScheduler"]
  ```

**For scipy-dependent schedulers (requires `is_torch_available() and is_scipy_available()`):**
- **Lines 116-118:** Special dependency
- **Pattern:**
  ```python
  _import_structure["scheduling_lms_discrete"] = ["LMSDiscreteScheduler"]
  ```

**For torchsde-dependent schedulers (requires `is_torch_available() and is_torchsde_available()`):**
- **Lines 127-129:** Special dependency
- **Pattern:**
  ```python
  _import_structure["scheduling_cosine_dpmsolver_multistep"] = ["CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler"]
  ```

#### **Section B: TYPE_CHECKING Actual Imports (Lines 131-223)**
Conditional actual imports for type checking and slow import:

**For PyTorch schedulers (Lines 146-186):**
```python
from .scheduling_YOUR_SCHEDULER_NAME import YourSchedulerClass
```
- **Location:** After line 146 (inside the `else` block for torch available)
- **Example:**
  ```python
  from .scheduling_ddim import DDIMScheduler
  ```

**For Flax schedulers (Lines 193-206):**
```python
from .scheduling_YOUR_SCHEDULER_NAME_flax import FlaxYourSchedulerClass
```

**For special dependencies (Lines 214, 222):**
Add similar imports in appropriate conditional blocks

---

### 3. **Top-Level Package Init File**
**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/__init__.py`

This re-exports schedulers at the package level. You must add entries in TWO places:

#### **Section A: Main _import_structure (Lines 341-384)**
**Lines 341-384:** Torch-dependent schedulers extended to the top-level import structure

```python
_import_structure["schedulers"].extend(
    [
        # ... existing entries in alphabetical order ...
        "YourSchedulerClass",
        # ...
    ]
)
```

**Exact location context from file (line 341 onwards):**
```python
_import_structure["schedulers"].extend(
    [
        "AmusedScheduler",
        # ... alphabetically sorted ...
        "VQDiffusionScheduler",
    ]
)
```

**For scipy/torchsde special cases:**
- **Line 398** (scipy): `_import_structure["schedulers"].extend(["LMSDiscreteScheduler"])`
- **Line 411** (torchsde): `_import_structure["schedulers"].extend(["CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler", "DPMSolverSDEScheduler"])`

#### **Section B: Flax Schedulers (Lines 817-829)**
Similar pattern for Flax:
```python
_import_structure["schedulers"].extend(
    [
        "FlaxDDIMScheduler",
        # ... other flax schedulers ...
    ]
)
```

#### **Section C: TYPE_CHECKING Imports (Lines 1112-1170)**
Two places where actual imports happen:

**Lines 1112-1153:** Main torch scheduler imports
```python
from .schedulers import (
    # ... existing imports ...
    YourSchedulerClass,
    # ...
)
```

**Lines 1528-1538:** Flax scheduler imports
```python
from .schedulers import (
    FlaxYourSchedulerClass,
    # ...
)
```

**Lines 1162, 1170:** Special dependency imports
```python
from .schedulers import LMSDiscreteScheduler  # for scipy
from .schedulers import CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler, DPMSolverSDEScheduler  # for torchsde
```

---

### 4. **KarrasDiffusionSchedulers Enum (OPTIONAL but RECOMMENDED)**
**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py`

**Lines 33-49:** If your scheduler is a "Karras-compatible" scheduler that should be listed as a compatible option for pipelines:

```python
class KarrasDiffusionSchedulers(Enum):
    DDIMScheduler = 1
    DDPMScheduler = 2
    # ... etc ...
    YourSchedulerClass = N  # Use next integer value
```

**Purpose:** This enum is used for pipeline compatibility declarations (the `_compatibles` class attribute in schedulers).

**For Flax equivalent:**
**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils_flax.py`

**Lines 36-43:** Same pattern for Flax:
```python
class FlaxKarrasDiffusionSchedulers(Enum):
    FlaxDDIMScheduler = 1
    # ...
    FlaxYourSchedulerClass = N
```

---

### 5. **Dummy Objects Files (IF OPTIONAL DEPENDENCIES)**
**Paths:** 
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/utils/dummy_pt_objects.py` (for torch-dependent)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/utils/dummy_flax_objects.py` (for flax-dependent)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/utils/dummy_torch_and_scipy_objects.py` (for scipy)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/utils/dummy_torch_and_torchsde_objects.py` (for torchsde)

**Note from file header:** These files are **auto-generated** by `make fix-copies` command. Do NOT edit manually. They contain dummy placeholder classes with the `DummyObject` metaclass for cases when dependencies aren't installed.

---

## SUMMARY TABLE

| File | Line(s) | Section | What to Add | Required |
|------|---------|---------|-------------|----------|
| `schedulers/__init__.py` | 32-82 | Lazy Import Structure | `_import_structure["scheduling_NAME"]` | YES |
| `schedulers/__init__.py` | 146-186 | TYPE_CHECKING imports | `from .scheduling_NAME import Class` | YES |
| `src/diffusers/__init__.py` | 341-384 | Top-level _import_structure | `"SchedulerClass"` in extend() | YES |
| `src/diffusers/__init__.py` | 1112-1153 | TYPE_CHECKING imports | `from .schedulers import Class` | YES |
| `scheduling_utils.py` | 33-49 | KarrasDiffusionSchedulers enum | Add enum entry | Optional* |
| `scheduling_utils_flax.py` | 36-43 | FlaxKarrasDiffusionSchedulers enum | Add enum entry | Optional* |
| `dummy_*.py` | N/A | Auto-generated | N/A | NO (auto-gen) |

*Optional but recommended if scheduler should be marked as compatible in pipelines

---

## NO EXPLICIT `__all__` LISTS

**Important finding:** The diffusers library does NOT use explicit `__all__` lists. It relies entirely on the `_import_structure` dictionary and the lazy module system (`_LazyModule`).

---

## KEY PATTERNS

1. **Module naming:** `scheduling_<descriptive_name>.py` format (e.g., `scheduling_ddim.py`)
2. **Class inheritance:** All torch schedulers inherit from `SchedulerMixin`; all flax from `FlaxSchedulerMixin`
3. **Alphabetical ordering:** Entries should be alphabetically sorted within each section
4. **Paired entries:** Torch and Flax versions use paired entries (same base name with `scheduling_<name>` vs `scheduling_<name>_flax`)
5. **No auto-registration:** There's NO automatic registry/metaclass system - all exports must be manually listed

---

## FILE ABSOLUTE PATHS (COMPLETE)

1. **Scheduler Implementation:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_YOUR_NAME.py`

2. **Schedulers Init (2 sections):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/__init__.py`

3. **Top-Level Init (3 sections):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/__init__.py`

4. **Utils (Optional):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py`

5. **Utils Flax (Optional):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils_flax.py`

### 3. Simple Scheduler Test Example: DDPM

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/tests/schedulers/test_scheduler_ddpm.py` (Lines 1-223)

**Structure:**
```python
from diffusers import DDPMScheduler
from .test_schedulers import SchedulerCommonTest

class DDPMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
    scheduler_classes = (DDPMScheduler,)
    
    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
        config = {
            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
            "beta_start": 0.0001,
            "beta_end": 0.02,
            "beta_schedule": "linear",
            "variance_type": "fixed_small",
            "clip_sample": True,
        }
        config.update(**kwargs)
        return config
```

**DDPM-Specific Tests (Lines 24-223):**
- `test_timesteps()` - Tests with [1, 5, 100, 1000] timesteps
- `test_betas()` - Tests with different beta_start/beta_end pairs
- `test_schedules()` - Tests with ["linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"] schedules
- `test_variance_type()` - Tests with ["fixed_small", "fixed_large", "other"]
- `test_clip_sample()` - Tests with [True, False]
- `test_thresholding()` - Tests thresholding with different prediction types
- `test_prediction_type()` - Tests ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]
- `test_time_indices()` - Tests with timesteps [0, 500, 999]
- `test_variance()` - Direct variance value assertions
- `test_rescale_betas_zero_snr()` - Tests zero SNR rescaling
- `test_full_loop_no_noise()` - Full generation loop, asserts output sum/mean
- `test_full_loop_with_v_prediction()` - Same with v_prediction type
- `test_custom_timesteps()` - Tests custom timestep scheduling
- `test_custom_timesteps_increasing_order()` - Tests error handling
- `test_custom_timesteps_passing_both_num_inference_steps_and_timesteps()` - Tests error handling
- `test_custom_timesteps_too_large()` - Tests error handling
- `test_full_loop_with_noise()` - Full loop with initial noise

### 6. Required Methods When Creating a New Scheduler Test

**MINIMAL REQUIRED:**
1. **Class Definition (inherit from SchedulerCommonTest):**

   class [SchedulerName]Test(SchedulerCommonTest):
       scheduler_classes = ([SchedulerClass],)  # Tuple of classes to test
   ```

2. **`get_scheduler_config()` Method:**
   - Must return a dict with all required scheduler initialization parameters
   - Must support `**kwargs` to allow test variations
   - Lines: typically 10-20 lines

**RECOMMENDED ADDITIONS:**
3. Parameter variation tests (test_timesteps, test_betas, test_prediction_type, etc.)
4. Full loop tests (test_full_loop_no_noise, test_full_loop_with_noise, etc.)
5. Full loop output assertions (sum and mean checks)
6. Scheduler-specific feature tests

**OPTIONAL ENHANCEMENTS:**
7. Helper methods like `full_loop(**config)` for reuse
8. Device-specific tests for GPU schedulers
9. Custom timestep/sigma tests

- `test_scheduler_ddim.py` → `DDIMScheduler`

```

### 9. Key Testing Utilities

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/tests/schedulers/test_schedulers.py`

**Base Test Classes:**
- `SchedulerBaseTests` (lines 101-253) - Config loading/saving tests
- `SchedulerCommonTest` (lines 256-807) - Common scheduler functionality tests
- `SchedulerPushToHubTester` (lines 811-867) - Hub integration tests (staging only)

**Common Testing Patterns:**
1. Use `self.check_over_configs(**config)` to test with config variations
2. Use `self.check_over_forward(**forward_kwargs)` to test with forward arg variations
3. Use `self.dummy_sample`, `self.dummy_noise_deter`, `self.dummy_sample_deter` for reproducibility
4. Use `torch.manual_seed(0)` for deterministic generator behavior
5. Assert on `torch.sum()` and `torch.mean()` of output for regression testing

### Summary Table: Pattern Comparison

| Aspect | DDPM | DDIM | Euler |
|---|---|---|---|
| Config Keys | 6 core | 6 core | 4 core |
| Forward Default Kwargs | None | (eta, num_inference_steps) | None |
| Full Loop Helper | No | Yes (full_loop) | Yes (3 variants) |
| Device Tests | No | No | Yes (2 methods) |
| Custom Timesteps Tests | Yes (4 methods) | No | Yes (2 methods) |
| Custom Sigmas Tests | No | No | Yes |
| Parameter Variation Tests | 7 test methods | 8 test methods | 8 test methods |
| Full Loop Tests | 4 methods | 4 methods | 6 methods |

This comprehensive infrastructure provides:
- Consistency across scheduler tests via base class inheritance
- Automatic testing of common functionality (save/load, output shapes, etc.)
- Clear patterns for adding scheduler-specific tests
- Deterministic testing with dummy models and fixed seeds
- Full integration with huggingface_hub for model saving/loading

- `test_prediction_type()` - Tests ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]
- `test_time_indices()` - Tests with timesteps [0, 500, 999]
- `test_variance()` - Direct variance value assertions
- `test_rescale_betas_zero_snr()` - Tests zero SNR rescaling
- `test_full_loop_no_noise()` - Full generation loop, asserts output sum/mean
- `test_full_loop_with_v_prediction()` - Same with v_prediction type
- `test_custom_timesteps()` - Tests custom timestep scheduling
- `test_custom_timesteps_increasing_order()` - Tests error handling
- `test_custom_timesteps_passing_both_num_inference_steps_and_timesteps()` - Tests error handling
- `test_custom_timesteps_too_large()` - Tests error handling
- `test_full_loop_with_noise()` - Full loop with initial noise

### 4. More Advanced Scheduler Test Example: Euler Discrete

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/tests/schedulers/test_scheduler_euler.py`

**Additional Patterns in Complex Schedulers:**

1. **Helper Methods for Full Loops:**
   ```python
   def full_loop(self, **config):  # Lines 52-73
       # Reusable full inference loop for testing
       
   def full_loop_custom_timesteps(self, **config):  # Lines 75-100
       # Tests with custom timestep scheduling
       
   def full_loop_custom_sigmas(self, **config):  # Lines 102-127
       # Tests with custom sigma scheduling
   ```

2. **Device-Specific Tests (Lines 147-199):**
   - `test_full_loop_device()` - GPU/device placement
   - `test_full_loop_device_karras_sigmas()` - Device placement with Karras sigmas

3. **Scheduler-Specific Features (Lines 24-71):**
   - `test_timestep_type()` - Tests ["discrete", "continuous"]
   - `test_karras_sigmas()` - Tests Karras sigma parameters
   - `test_beta_sigmas()` - Tests beta sigma options
   - `test_exponential_sigmas()` - Tests exponential sigma options

### 5. Intermediate Scheduler Test Example: DDIM

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/tests/schedulers/test_scheduler_ddim.py` (Lines 1-177)

**Key Pattern Differences:**

1. **Forward Default Kwargs (Line 10):**
   ```python
   forward_default_kwargs = (("eta", 0.0), ("num_inference_steps", 50))
   ```
   This defines kwargs that are always passed to `scheduler.step()`

2. **Full Loop Helper with Custom Config (Lines 24-40):**
   - Reusable pattern for deterministic testing
   - Allows configuration variants to be tested

3. **DDIM-Specific Tests:**
   - `test_steps_offset()` - Tests timestep offset behavior
   - `test_timestep_spacing()` - Tests ["trailing", "leading"] spacing
   - `test_inference_steps()` - Tests different inference step counts
   - `test_eta()` - Tests stochasticity parameter (eta)

### 6. Required Methods When Creating a New Scheduler Test

**MINIMAL REQUIRED:**
1. **Class Definition (inherit from SchedulerCommonTest):**
   ```python
   class [SchedulerName]Test(SchedulerCommonTest):
       scheduler_classes = ([SchedulerClass],)  # Tuple of classes to test
   ```

2. **`get_scheduler_config()` Method:**
   - Must return a dict with all required scheduler initialization parameters
   - Must support `**kwargs` to allow test variations
   - Lines: typically 10-20 lines

**RECOMMENDED ADDITIONS:**
3. Parameter variation tests (test_timesteps, test_betas, test_prediction_type, etc.)
4. Full loop tests (test_full_loop_no_noise, test_full_loop_with_noise, etc.)
5. Full loop output assertions (sum and mean checks)
6. Scheduler-specific feature tests

**OPTIONAL ENHANCEMENTS:**
7. Helper methods like `full_loop(**config)` for reuse
8. Device-specific tests for GPU schedulers
9. Custom timestep/sigma tests
10. Deprecated behavior tests

### 7. Test File Naming Convention

**Pattern:** `test_scheduler_[scheduler_name].py`

**Examples:**
- `test_scheduler_ddpm.py` → `DDPMScheduler`
- `test_scheduler_ddim.py` → `DDIMScheduler`
- `test_scheduler_euler.py` → `EulerDiscreteScheduler`
- `test_scheduler_dpm_multi.py` → `DPMSolverMultistepScheduler`
- `test_scheduler_unipc.py` → `UniPCMultistepScheduler`

**Class Naming:** `[SchedulerClassCamelCase]Test`

### 8. All Available Scheduler Tests (30 Files)

```
test_scheduler_consistency_model.py
test_scheduler_ddim.py
test_scheduler_ddim_inverse.py
test_scheduler_ddim_parallel.py
test_scheduler_ddpm.py
test_scheduler_ddpm_parallel.py
test_scheduler_deis.py
test_scheduler_dpm_multi.py
test_scheduler_dpm_multi_inverse.py
test_scheduler_dpm_sde.py
test_scheduler_dpm_single.py
test_scheduler_edm_dpmsolver_multistep.py
test_scheduler_edm_euler.py
test_scheduler_euler.py
test_scheduler_euler_ancestral.py
test_scheduler_heun.py
test_scheduler_ipndm.py
test_scheduler_kdpm2_ancestral.py
test_scheduler_kdpm2_discrete.py
test_scheduler_lcm.py
test_scheduler_lms.py
test_scheduler_pndm.py
test_scheduler_sasolver.py
test_scheduler_score_sde_ve.py
test_scheduler_tcd.py
test_scheduler_unclip.py
test_scheduler_unipc.py
test_scheduler_vq_diffusion.py
```

### 9. Key Testing Utilities

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775499603007-kfs0b3/tests/schedulers/test_schedulers.py`

**Base Test Classes:**
- `SchedulerBaseTests` (lines 101-253) - Config loading/saving tests
- `SchedulerCommonTest` (lines 256-807) - Common scheduler functionality tests
- `SchedulerPushToHubTester` (lines 811-867) - Hub integration tests (staging only)

**Common Testing Patterns:**
1. Use `self.check_over_configs(**config)` to test with config variations
2. Use `self.check_over_forward(**forward_kwargs)` to test with forward arg variations
3. Use `self.dummy_sample`, `self.dummy_noise_deter`, `self.dummy_sample_deter` for reproducibility
4. Use `torch.manual_seed(0)` for deterministic generator behavior
5. Assert on `torch.sum()` and `torch.mean()` of output for regression testing

### Summary Table: Pattern Comparison

| Aspect | DDPM | DDIM | Euler |
|---|---|---|---|
| Config Keys | 6 core | 6 core | 4 core |
| Forward Default Kwargs | None | (eta, num_inference_steps) | None |
| Full Loop Helper | No | Yes (full_loop) | Yes (3 variants) |
| Device Tests | No | No | Yes (2 methods) |
| Custom Timesteps Tests | Yes (4 methods) | No | Yes (2 methods) |
| Custom Sigmas Tests | No | No | Yes |
| Parameter Variation Tests | 7 test methods | 8 test methods | 8 test methods |
| Full Loop Tests | 4 methods | 4 methods | 6 methods |

This comprehensive infrastructure provides:
- Consistency across scheduler tests via base class inheritance
- Automatic testing of common functionality (save/load, output shapes, etc.)
- Clear patterns for adding scheduler-specific tests
- Deterministic testing with dummy models and fixed seeds
- Full integration with huggingface_hub for model saving/loading

import math
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Literal

import numpy as np
import torch

from ..configuration_utils import ConfigMixin, register_to_config
from ..utils import BaseOutput
from ..utils.torch_utils import randn_tensor
from .scheduling_utils import KarrasDiffusionSchedulers, SchedulerMixin


@dataclass
class DDPMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    """
    Output class for the scheduler's `step` function output.

    Args:
        prev_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
            Computed sample `(x_{t-1})` of previous timestep. `prev_sample` should be used as next model input in the
            denoising loop.
        pred_original_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
            The predicted denoised sample `(x_{0})` based on the model output from the current timestep.
            `pred_original_sample` can be used to preview progress or for guidance.
    """

    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None


def betas_for_alpha_bar(
    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
    max_beta: float = 0.999,
    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function, which defines the cumulative product of
    (1-beta) over time from t = [0,1].

    Contains a function alpha_bar that takes an argument t and transforms it to the cumulative product of (1-beta) up
    to that part of the diffusion process.

    Args:
        num_diffusion_timesteps (`int`):
            The number of betas to produce.
        max_beta (`float`, defaults to `0.999`):
            The maximum beta to use; use values lower than 1 to avoid numerical instability.
        alpha_transform_type (`str`, defaults to `"cosine"`):
            The type of noise schedule for `alpha_bar`. Choose from `cosine`, `exp`, or `laplace`.

    Returns:
        `torch.Tensor`:
            The betas used by the scheduler to step the model outputs.
    """
    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":

        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2

    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":

        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
            snr = math.exp(lmb)
            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))

    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":

        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
            return math.exp(t * -12.0)

    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")

    betas = []
    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)


# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddim.rescale_zero_terminal_snr

113	                      configuration saved with [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`].

117	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.

127	                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.

141	        with `hf > auth login`. You can also activate the special >

import importlib
import os
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum

import torch
from huggingface_hub.utils import validate_hf_hub_args
from typing_extensions import Self

from ..utils import BaseOutput, PushToHubMixin


SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME = "scheduler_config.json"


# NOTE: We make this type an enum because it simplifies usage in docs and prevents
# circular imports when used for `_compatibles` within the schedulers module.
# When it's used as a type in pipelines, it really is a Union because the actual
# scheduler instance is passed in.
class KarrasDiffusionSchedulers(Enum):
    DDIMScheduler = 1
    DDPMScheduler = 2
    PNDMScheduler = 3
    LMSDiscreteScheduler = 4
    EulerDiscreteScheduler = 5
    HeunDiscreteScheduler = 6
    EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler = 7
    DPMSolverMultistepScheduler = 8
    DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler = 9
    KDPM2DiscreteScheduler = 10
    KDPM2AncestralDiscreteScheduler = 11
    DEISMultistepScheduler = 12
    UniPCMultistepScheduler = 13
    DPMSolverSDEScheduler = 14
    EDMEulerScheduler = 15


AysSchedules = {
    "StableDiffusionTimesteps": [999, 850, 736, 645, 545, 455, 343, 233, 124, 24],
    "StableDiffusionSigmas": [14.615, 6.475, 3.861, 2.697, 1.886, 1.396, 0.963, 0.652, 0.399, 0.152, 0.0],
    "StableDiffusionXLTimesteps": [999, 845, 730, 587, 443, 310, 193, 116, 53, 13],
    "StableDiffusionXLSigmas": [14.615, 6.315, 3.771, 2.181, 1.342, 0.862, 0.555, 0.380, 0.234, 0.113, 0.0],
    "StableDiffusionVideoSigmas": [700.00, 54.5, 15.886, 7.977, 4.248, 1.789, 0.981, 0.403, 0.173, 0.034, 0.0],
}


@dataclass
class SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    """
    Base class for the output of a scheduler's `step` function.

    Args:
        prev_sample (`torch.Tensor` of shape `(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)` for images):
            Computed sample `(x_{t-1})` of previous timestep. `prev_sample` should be used as next model input in the
            denoising loop.
    """

    prev_sample: torch.Tensor


class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
    """
    Base class for all schedulers.

    [`SchedulerMixin`] contains common functions shared by all schedulers such as general loading and saving
    functionalities.

    [`ConfigMixin`] takes care of storing the configuration attributes (like `num_train_timesteps`) that are passed to
    the scheduler's `__init__` function, and the attributes can be accessed by `scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps`.

    Class attributes:
        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of scheduler classes that are compatible with the parent scheduler
          class. Use [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] to load a different compatible scheduler class (should be overridden
          by parent class).
    """

    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME
    _compatibles = []
    has_compatibles = True

    @classmethod
    @validate_hf_hub_args
    def from_pretrained(
        cls,
        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
        subfolder: str | None = None,
        return_unused_kwargs=False,
        **kwargs,
    ) -> Self:
        r"""
        Instantiate a scheduler from a pre-defined JSON configuration file in a local directory or Hub repository.

        Parameters:
            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
                Can be either:

                    - A string, the *model id* (for example `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`) of a pretrained model hosted on
                      the Hub.
                    - A path to a *directory* (for example `./my_model_directory`) containing the scheduler
                      configuration saved with [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`].
            subfolder (`str`, *optional*):
                The subfolder location of a model file within a larger model repository on the Hub or locally.
            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
                Path to a directory where a downloaded pretrained model configuration is cached if the standard cache
                is not used.
            force_download (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
                cached versions if they exist.

            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):
                A dictionary of proxy servers to use by protocol or endpoint, for example, `{'http': 'foo.bar:3128',
                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.
            output_loading_info(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether or not to also return a dictionary containing missing keys, unexpected keys and error messages.
            local_files_only(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether to only load local model weights and configuration files or not. If set to `True`, the model
                won't be downloaded from the Hub.
            token (`str` or *bool*, *optional*):
                The token to use as HTTP bearer authorization for remote files. If `True`, the token generated from
                `diffusers-cli login` (stored in `~/.huggingface`) is used.
            revision (`str`, *optional*, defaults to `"main"`):
                The specific model version to use. It can be a branch name, a tag name, a commit id, or any identifier
                allowed by Git.

        > [!TIP] > To use private or [gated models](https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/models-gated#gated-models), log-in
        with `hf > auth login`. You can also activate the special >
        ["offline-mode"](https://huggingface.co/diffusers/installation.html#offline-mode) to use this method in a >
        firewalled environment.

        """
        config, kwargs, commit_hash = cls.load_config(
            pretrained_model_name_or_path=pretrained_model_name_or_path,
            subfolder=subfolder,
            return_unused_kwargs=True,
            return_commit_hash=True,
            **kwargs,
        )
        return cls.from_config(config, return_unused_kwargs=return_unused_kwargs, **kwargs)

    def save_pretrained(self, save_directory: str | os.PathLike, push_to_hub: bool = False, **kwargs):
        """
        Save a scheduler configuration object to a directory so that it can be reloaded using the
        [`~SchedulerMixin.from_pretrained`] class method.

        Args:
            save_directory (`str` or `os.PathLike`):
                Directory where the configuration JSON file will be saved (will be created if it does not exist).
            push_to_hub (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
                Whether or not to push your model to the Hugging Face Hub after saving it. You can specify the
                repository you want to push to with `repo_id` (will default to the name of `save_directory` in your
                namespace).
            kwargs (`dict[str, Any]`, *optional*):
                Additional keyword arguments passed along to the [`~utils.PushToHubMixin.push_to_hub`] method.
        """
        self.save_config(save_directory=save_directory, push_to_hub=push_to_hub, **kwargs)

    @property
    def compatibles(self):
        """
        Returns all schedulers that are compatible with this scheduler

        Returns:
            `list[SchedulerMixin]`: list of compatible schedulers
        """
        return self._get_compatibles()

    @classmethod
    def _get_compatibles(cls):
        compatible_classes_str = list(set([cls.__name__] + cls._compatibles))
        diffusers_library = importlib.import_module(__name__.split(".")[0])
        compatible_classes = [
            getattr(diffusers_library, c) for c in compatible_classes_str if hasattr(diffusers_library, c)
        ]
        return compatible_classes

100	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddim.rescale_zero_terminal_snr
101	def rescale_zero_terminal_snr(betas: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
102	    """
103	    Rescales betas to have zero terminal SNR Based on https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891 (Algorithm 1)
104	
105	    Args:
106	        betas (`torch.Tensor`):
107	            The betas that the scheduler is being initialized with.
108	
109	    Returns:
110	        `torch.Tensor`:
111	            Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR.
112	    """
113	    # Convert betas to alphas_bar_sqrt
114	    alphas = 1.0 - betas
115	    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
116	    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
117	
118	    # Store old values.
119	    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
120	    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
121	
122	    # Shift so the last timestep is zero.
123	    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
124	
125	    # Scale so the first timestep is back to the old value.
126	    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
127	
128	    # Convert alphas_bar_sqrt to betas
129	    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2  # Revert sqrt
130	    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]  # Revert cumprod
131	    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
132	    betas = 1 - alphas
133	
134	    return betas
